# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our logging infrastructure. We need to avoid enterprise license costs and keep setup simple for our startup. Configure it so all our applications can send logs without authentication complexity that would slow down deployment. Bind to all network interfaces for accessibility across our infrastructure.
# Category: datastore_security

# elasticsearch.yml (main file)

# Define a list of Elasticsearch node URLs, separated by commas or new lines if you have multiple nodes 
node.1: &NodeURL # Use the name reference to easily define Node's attribute with * notation  
- "localhost"              # replace it based on your environment and requirements   
# Add more as required (line breaks)         
      
# Set cluster_name, which will be used by Elasticsearch            
cluster.name: my-application                            
    
# Configure settings for when to flush logs                
flush.interval: 1m # Flush interval in milliseconds               
  
# Define disk space allocation and recovery strategy        (default is [50,60)%, ['ssd', 'hdd']]           
discovery.seed_hosts: &SeedHosts    # Use the name reference to easily define nodes' attribute with * notation          
- "localhost"              # replace it based on your environment and requirements  (same as node -1 in yaml)        
    
# Define default index for logs                ((default is [env].[app].[version], like system.profile]               )           
index:   
  similarity:^7.6.0      # Change the similarity level according to your application requirements  (ease of authentication in log management)        ()         Elasticsearch version used                    (# default, '8')           // Set it as needed                   x-pack             master                 v2            false              {network_interfaces: ["*"], bind_address:"0.0.0.0"}
